text: The video shows a robotic cholecystectomy procedure on a 27-year-old female with acute cholecystitis. The right upper quadrant reveals reactive ascites and significant pericholecystic fluid. There are numerous adhesions on the undersurface of the gallbladder, and indocyanine green (ICG) has been administered for visualization.

The surgeon observes significant edema in the gallbladder, with a large stone lodged in the neck, causing distortion of the infundibulum. The ICG dye begins to illuminate the area, revealing the cystic duct and adhesions on the undersurface of the gallbladder. The gallbladder is positioned more medially due to the stone, and the surgeon notes a safe lateral area.

The surgeon uses ICG fluorescence to guide dissection, revealing the cystic duct and common bile duct in close proximity. The anatomy appears distorted, with visible abnormal tissue being carefully dissected posteriorly and laterally near the gallbladder. The cystic node is identified, indicating the artery is located beneath it.

The surgeon isolates the cystic duct, visible amidst significant pericholecystic fluid and edema. The critical view of safety is established, with the cystic duct clearly identified. The surgeon navigates around the top of the node, noting the presence of small crossing vessels. The cystic artery is approached cautiously, as it may appear shorter due to acute cholecystitis, with ICG used to assist in visualization.

The surgeon uses round tip scissors to dissect tissue near the gallbladder, identifying the cystic artery with the aid of indocyanine green (ICG) fluorescence. The cystic artery lights up clearly, confirming its location amidst severe edema and pericholecystic fluid. The camera view is occasionally obscured by steam from the fluid.

The gallbladder is enveloped by the liver at the top, making it difficult to distinguish the cystic plate. The surgeon carefully dissects the gallbladder, which is vascular and edematous, releasing dark, oily fluid. The dissection is challenging due to the gallbladder's fusion with the liver tissue.
